brief_justification: Arrives at exactly D (12.9%) via correct Bayes' theorem application; identifies base rate neglect fallacy, explains the conditional probability inversion, and contextualizes the signal strength — three analytical levels; names the analyst's fallacy but does not audit its own reasoning process or check assumptions mid-derivation; rechecks arithmetic implicitly through the step-by-step derivation but no independent re-derivation or boundary test; presents the answer with full confidence and no acknowledgment of data limitations or what assumptions underlie the calculation; explains why 80% (analyst error) is wrong and notes 10% as the base rate, but does not formally evaluate all answer choices as competing hypotheses; every intermediate step is shown with explicit numbers and formulas — a third party can fully reproduce the calculation.
